name: Asparagus Flan (July 1997)
recipeIngredient: salt and white pepper to taste
salt and white pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate asparagus tips (about 2").
2. Cook asparagus tips and the rest of the stalks in a little salted water for a few minutes. They should remain firm. Drain the tips.
3. Cook the rest of the asparagus another 5 minutes. Drain. Slice half of the stalks.
4. Cook the shallots in 1 tablespoon butter until wilted. Let cool.
5. Process the remaining stalks (not the tips) along with the cooked shallots until they become a paste. Add tarragon, eggs, pepper, and salt; process to blend.
6. Add cream to the food processor and process for ½ minute.
7. Butter 6 ramekins and divide the sliced stalks among them.
8. Pour the egg mixture into the ramekins. Place the ramekins in a pan with water and bake at 300°F for 40 minutes or until firm.
9. Meanwhile, prepare the sauce:
10. Reduce the wine with 1 teaspoon tarragon and white pepper to ½ tablespoon over low heat. Slowly add butter 1 tablespoon at a time, whisking, until all the butter has been incorporated. Check seasoning.
11. Unmold the flans onto individual plates, decorate with the tips, and pour the sauce over the tips.
